executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 298,308.96 inc VAT (271,550.61 ex VAT) for the week ending 13 June 2026.
Overall margin finished at 18.55%, which should be reviewed in the context of department-level data anomalies across the fresh and produce categories.
Total waste of 8,719.80 represents a waste percentage of 3.21% of sales ex VAT, which is elevated and requires immediate attention across multiple departments.

Several departments are reporting margins well in excess of 100%, most notably Produce at 132.51%, Beef at 104.71%, and Fish at 100.71%, indicating likely stock count, opening stock, or purchase data issues that must be investigated before these figures are accepted.

The fresh food departments — Fish (1,306.99), Deli (1,359.55), Poultry (1,031.56), and Provisions (1,006.37) — are the four largest individual waste contributors, collectively accounting for 4,704.47 of the total 8,719.80 waste.

Pork is the only department reporting a realistic cost of sales and a below-scan margin at 64.48% versus a scan margin of 18.68%, which points to a pricing, shrink, or purchasing issue requiring review.

Cigarettes and Deli are both reporting 100.0% margin, which in the case of Cigarettes reflects the department's standard reporting treatment, but Deli at 100.0% with no cost of sales data recorded suggests a data capture failure.

The TOTAL department row shows purchases of 0.00 and a combined negative cost of sales of -7,560.07, confirming that purchase data is missing across most departments and that this week's P&L cannot be taken at face value without reconciliation.
biggest_wins: Poultry delivered sales of 8,220.26 with a scan margin of 16.05%, and despite waste of 1,031.56, the department generated a gross profit of 7,579.93, making it one of the strongest volume contributors in the fresh category.
Produce achieved sales of 25,079.72, the highest of any single department in the report, with a scan margin of 20.40%, underpinning its central role in store turnover.

Provisions posted sales of 19,000.92 with a scan margin of 21.90%, the highest scan margin among the ambient and chilled provisions departments, and waste of 1,006.37 remains the primary area to address.
Deli recorded sales of 11,463.69 with a theoretical scan margin of 71.71%, which, if validated by accurate cost data, would represent a significant gross profit contributor to the store.
Beef generated sales of 10,969.49 with a scan margin of 16.38%, and the opening stock of 6,600.05 versus closing stock of 7,116.87 suggests healthy stock levels going into the following week.
biggest_risks: Produce is reporting a closing stock of 8,152.47 with no opening stock or purchases recorded, producing a negative cost of sales of -8,152.47 and an inflated margin of 132.51%. This is a data integrity issue and must be corrected before any margin conclusion is drawn. Produce waste of 1,515.21 also demands attention.

Fish waste of 1,306.99 against sales of 2,220.01 represents a waste-to-sales ratio of approximately 58.9% for the department, which is commercially unsustainable and requires an immediate review of ordering volumes, sell-by date management, and markdown activity.

Deli waste of 1,359.55 against sales of 11,463.69 equates to approximately 11.9% waste within the department. With no cost of sales data available, the true margin is unknown and the business is exposed.

Provisions waste of 1,006.37 against sales of 19,000.92 equates to approximately 5.3% waste. Given the ambient nature of much of the provisions range, this level of waste is high and suggests ordering discipline or date rotation issues.

Pork is the only department with a reported margin of 64.48% against a scan margin of 18.68%, meaning actual margin is materially below theoretical. With a cost of sales of 485.85 on sales of 1,367.73, the gap versus scan needs to be investigated for pricing errors, till keying issues, or unrecorded waste.

Bread and Cakes waste of 1,003.18 is reported in the waste breakdown but does not appear as a standalone department in the P&L, meaning this waste may not be fully reflected in the department margin and requires reconciliation.
Dairy waste of 491.53 and Frozen waste of 63.64 are similarly recorded in the waste breakdown without corresponding P&L department lines, creating a gap in the store's full margin picture.

Purchase data is missing across all major departments in the P&L (total purchases recorded as 0.00). Until purchase figures are loaded and reconciled, the reported department margins are unreliable and should not be used for trading decisions.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Sales of 10,670.85 with a scan margin of 9.68%. The 100.0% reported margin is a standard artefact of how the department is set up in the system and does not reflect true profitability. No anomaly beyond data treatment.

Beef: Sales of 10,969.49, scan margin 16.38%. Reported margin of 104.71% and negative cost of sales of -516.82 indicate that closing stock of 7,116.87 has increased over opening stock of 6,600.05 with no purchases recorded. Purchases must be entered to produce a valid margin. Waste of 594.35 should be monitored.

Lamb: Sales of 984.76, scan margin 24.75%. Reported margin of 103.04% with a negative cost of sales of -29.95 reflects a minor stock build and absent purchase data. Low volume department but waste of 149.87 is proportionally significant at approximately 15.2% of sales.

Pork: Sales of 1,367.73, scan margin 18.68%. This is the only department with a credible-looking cost of sales (485.85), yet the resulting margin of 64.48% is far below scan. Requires investigation into pricing, waste recording, or purchase allocation. Waste of 99.78 noted.

Bacon and Loose SRP: Sales of 1,349.36, scan margin 13.94%. Reported margin of 97.81% with a cost of sales of only 29.50 against sales of 1,349.36 is not credible in isolation and is likely the result of missing purchase data. Waste of 97.78 should be reviewed.

Poultry: Sales of 8,220.26, scan margin 16.05%. Reported margin of 92.21% reflects missing purchase inputs. Waste of 1,031.56 is the second highest in the fresh category and represents approximately 12.5% of department sales, which is a key area for action.

Fish: Sales of 2,220.01, scan margin 15.21%. Reported margin of 100.71% masks the real issue, which is waste of 1,306.99 representing approximately 58.9% of sales. Ordering and markdown protocols need urgent review.

Produce: Sales of 25,079.72, scan margin 20.40%. Closing stock of 8,152.47 with no opening stock or purchases creates a negative cost of sales of -8,152.47 and an inflated margin of 132.51%. Data must be corrected. Waste of 1,515.21 is the highest single waste line in the store.

Deli: Sales of 11,463.69, scan margin 71.71%. No cost of sales data is present, resulting in a 100.0% reported margin. The scan margin of 71.71% is strong if accurate, but the absence of any cost recording means this cannot be confirmed. Waste of 1,359.55 is high.

Provisions: Sales of 19,000.92, scan margin 21.90%. Reported margin of 100.0% with a negligible cost of sales movement (opening 23.33, closing 24.18, cos -0.85) confirms purchases are not recorded. Waste of 1,006.37 at approximately 5.3% of sales is above acceptable levels for this category.
action_plan: Escalate the missing purchase data issue to the finance and IT teams immediately. No valid P&L can be produced for Week Ending 13 June 2026 until purchase figures are entered and reconciled across all departments, particularly Produce, Beef, Poultry, Provisions, Deli, Fish, Lamb, and Bacon.

Investigate the Produce department opening stock omission. The absence of an opening stock figure combined with a closing stock of 8,152.47 and no purchases is producing a false margin of 132.51%. Confirm whether a stock count was completed and ensure the figure is posted correctly.

Conduct an immediate review of Fish ordering and waste management. Waste of 1,306.99 against sales of 2,220.01 is not commercially viable. The department manager must review daily order quantities, sell-by date rotation, and the frequency and timing of markdown activity.

Review Deli waste of 1,359.55 and implement a daily production planning review. Deli managers should be tracking end-of-day surplus against production records to reduce over-production. A target waste reduction of 20% week on week should be set.

Investigate Pork margin shortfall. The reported margin of 64.48% against a scan margin of 18.68% is the widest adverse gap between actual and theoretical in the store. Check for till scanning errors, incorrect pricing, unrecorded waste, or purchase misallocation.

Review Poultry waste of 1,031.56, which represents approximately 12.5% of department sales. Confirm that daily waste is being recorded accurately, that orders are being adjusted based on actual sales patterns, and that all dated stock is being actioned through markdown or removal.

Address Provisions waste of 1,006.37. Review the range for any lines with consistently poor sell-through, check date rotation compliance on all chilled provisions, and confirm that waste is being recorded at the point of removal rather than retrospectively.

Reconcile Bread and Cakes waste of 1,003.18 and Dairy waste of 491.53 to their respective P&L department lines. These categories are recording waste without a corresponding department margin entry, which means the store's true margin is understated and the waste is not being reported in context.

Carry out a full stock count verification for Beef (closing stock 7,116.87) and Poultry (closing stock 2,754.95) to confirm that counted figures are accurate and that the variance between opening and closing stock is genuine rather than a counting error.

Set a store-wide waste reduction target for Week Ending 20 June 2026. Total waste of 8,719.80 at 3.21% is above the acceptable threshold. Department managers should be briefed individually on their waste figures and asked to submit a written action plan by close of business Monday.

Ensure Deli cost of sales data is captured correctly in next week's P&L. A scan margin of 71.71% is the highest in the store and the absence of any cost recording means the department's true contribution cannot be assessed or managed.

Schedule a department manager P&L review meeting for early in the week of 15 June 2026 to walk through this week's figures, address data gaps, and set clear targets for margin, waste, and ordering accuracy ahead of the Week Ending 20 June 2026 close.
